topic: A person discovers their partner has been taking secret phone calls
raw_output:
[story 1]

I found the phone tucked deep inside the lining of his old jacket pocket. It wasn't a burner, not exactly, but it had a pattern of recent, unanswered calls that made my stomach clench. I held it, the cool plastic a stark contrast to the sudden heat rising in my cheeks. He was meticulous about his phone usage, always keeping it close, but this… this felt like a carefully constructed secret I had stumbled upon. I traced the screen, the numbers blurring into meaningless strings. When he walked in, whistling off-key, I didn't say anything immediately. I just held up the device, my hand trembling slightly. His casual demeanor evaporated instantly. His eyes widened, a flicker of something sharp and defensive crossing his face before he tried to smoothly take the phone back, his voice tight.

[story 2]

The silence in the apartment was thick enough to choke on. I’d noticed the pattern myself—the sudden urgency in his voice when he stepped out of the room, the way he’d subtly shield his phone when I glanced over. Tonight, it was undeniable. I saw the notification light flash on the screen just as he was settling onto the sofa, and a knot tightened painfully in my chest. I didn't confront him right away. Instead, I started cleaning, scrubbing the kitchen counters with unnecessary vigor, my movements jerky and sharp. Every sound seemed amplified in the quiet space. When he finally asked what was wrong, his tone was laced with a strained patience that made my jaw ache. I just shook my head, unable to articulate the dizzying sense of dislocation washing over me.

[story 3]

It happened during a routine check of his bag. I found it nestled beneath a pair of socks, the familiar shape of the device immediately signaling trouble. My breath hitched. I didn't move for a full minute, just staring at it, my mind racing through impossible scenarios. What was he hiding? Why the furtive glances when he answered it? When I finally turned to him, the air between us felt electrically charged. I didn't raise my voice; instead, I spoke in a low, flat tone, the sound barely cutting through the tension. "Who are you talking to?" The question hung there, heavy and accusing. His composure crumbled. He looked away, his shoulders slumping, and I could see the effort it took for him to simply exist in the same room as me again.
